title: 'Take Up Thy Cross', the Savior Said
abc_source:
X: 296
T: 'Take Up Thy Cross', the Savior Said
%%combinevoices 1
C: Words: Charles W. Everest, 1833.  Music: 'Nun Lasst Uns Den Leib Begraben' George Rhau, 1544.  
C: Setting: "Evangelical Lutheran Hymn-Book", 1931.

w: 1.~"Take up thy cross," the Sa- vior said, "If thou wouldst My dis- ci- ple be;

w: 3.~Take up thy cross, nor heed the shame, Nor let thy fool- ish pride re- bel; 
w: 4.~Take up thy cross then in His strength, And calm- ly sin's wild de- luge brave, 
w: 5.~Take up thy cross and fol- low Christ, Nor think til death to lay it down;

w: De- ny thy- self, the world for- sake, And hum- bly fol- low af- ter Me." 
w: His strength shall bear thy spi- rit up, And brace thy heart and nerve thine arm. 
w: Thy Lord for thee the cross en- dured, And saved thy soul from death and hell. 
w: 'Twill guide thee to a bet- ter home, It points to glo- ry o'er the grave. 
w: For on- ly those who bear the cross May hope to wear the glor- ious crown.

% 12
W: 6.To Thee, great Lord, the One in Three,
W: All praise forevermore ascend:
W: O grant us in our home to see
W: The heavenly life that knows no end.

metadata.composer: 'Nun Lasst Uns Den Leib Begraben' George Rhau, 1544 "Evangelical Lutheran Hymn-Book", 1931
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Charles W. Everest, 1833.  Music: 'Nun Lasst Uns Den Leib Begraben' George Rhau, 1544.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2006 Revision.

1.raw_text: "Take up thy cross," the Savior said, "If thou wouldst My disciple be; Deny thyself, the world forsake, And humbly follow after Me."

2.raw_text: Take up thy cross, let not its weight Fill thy weak spirit with alarm; His strength shall bear thy spirit up, And brace thy heart and nerve thine arm.

3.raw_text: Take up thy cross, nor heed the shame, Nor let thy foolish pride rebel; Thy Lord for thee the cross endured, And saved thy soul from death and hell.

4.raw_text: Take up thy cross then in His strength, And calmly sin's wild deluge brave, 'Twill guide thee to a better home, It points to glory o'er the grave.

5.raw_text: Take up thy cross and follow Christ, Nor think til death to lay it down; For only those who bear the cross May hope to wear the glorious crown.
